name: UserAdd
documentation: be "none", as this represents the NULL-pointer when dealing with groups. He has default rights, determined by umask. Returns: 0 - on success 1 - he is already there -1 - he still does not exist (didn't get memory) =====================================================================

name: UserDel
documentation: UserDel- Remove a user from the list. Destroy all his permissions and completely detach him from the session. Returns 0 - success -1 - he who does not exist cannot be removed =====================================================================

name: UserFreeCopyBuffer
documentation: UserFreeCopyBuffer- frees user buffer Also removes any references into the users copybuffer Returns: 0 - if the copy buffer was really deleted. -1 - cannot remove something that does not exist =====================================================================

name: FindSocket
documentation: The first good socket is stored in fdp and its name is appended to SocketPath. If none exists or fdp is NULL SocketPath is not changed. Returns: number of good sockets.

name: wmb_create
documentation: Allocate and initialize to the empty string a new window message buffer. The return value must be freed using wmbc_free.

name: wmb_expand
documentation: Attempts to expand the buffer to hold at least MIN bytes. The new size of the buffer is returned, which may be unchanged from the original size if additional memory could not be allocated.

name: wmb_free
documentation: Deinitialize and free memory allocated to the given window buffer

name: wmbc_create
documentation: Allocate and initialize a buffer context for the given buffer. The return value must be freed using wmbc_free.

name: wmbc_free
documentation: Deinitializes and frees previously allocated context. The contained buffer must be freed separately; this function will not do so for you.
